channel.title: qwertymarmot.github.io
channel.description: Sharing knowledge is the most fundamental act of friendship. Because it is a way you can give something without loosing something by Richard Stallman

item.title: Our decisions impact what is important to us
item.description:
Notes:

There is never enough time for us to pursue every opportunity available to us. We are forced to make decisions. Each time we make a decision to do something we are casting our vote on what we most value. We must make sure that these decisions are in line with what is most important to us so that we live in our values. Otherwise we are just putting up a front.

Source:

Farnam Street Brain Food Newsletter

item.title: How social circles impact the paths of life
item.description:
Notes:

Our lives tend to take a the path of least resistence. Certain factors in our lives can heavily influence the likely path our lives will take. These factors include;

  • The family we are born into
  • The high school we attend
  • The college we attend
  • Our first job
  • Marriage

For example if we go to a college where most of the graduates end up in the finance industry it is very likely we will also be influenced to pursue a career in the finance industry. These paths help to explain the network effects that allow the web if influence in the upper class to allow their immediate familes to continue down the path of upper class status and receive the preferential treatment and opportunties that apply to that group.

Source:

Your Life is Driven by Network Effects. (2020, February 25). NFX. https://www.nfx.com/post/your-life-network-effects/

See Also

Confirmation Bias
